message:
fcp-sdk: fail-closed on unregistered rate-limit pool (br-83xt1)

RateLimitTracker::try_consume previously iterated an operation's
referenced pool ids and silently skipped any pool_id that wasn't
present in `pools`. That's fail-open: if operation_map references
a pool that was never added, every request for that operation slips
through throttling.

Fix: add an up-front guard that rejects with a hard RateLimitError
naming both the missing pool and the operation when any referenced
pool is absent. The guard runs before the two-phase check/consume
so registered pools are not side-effected when a sibling pool is
missing (verified in try_consume_partial_missing_pool_fails_closed).

Current call sites are safe in production because
RateLimitDeclarations::validate() catches UnknownPool references
at manifest parse time, but:
  - from_declarations() does NOT call validate() internally.
  - Callers that assemble decls by hand or reconfigure at runtime
    (add_pool/remove_pool + mutate operation_map) can drift into an
    inconsistent state.

Fail-closed is the right default for an admission-control primitive;
an inconsistency should surface as a configuration error, not a
silent pass.

Tests:
- try_consume_fails_closed_on_unregistered_pool: the motivating case.
- try_consume_unknown_operation_still_returns_none: preserves the
  distinct "operation not in map" behavior (tracker has no opinion).
- try_consume_partial_missing_pool_fails_closed: proves the registered
  pool's counter is NOT consumed when a sibling pool is missing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
